specific_mechanism: Coins are struck between two engraved dies (obverse and reverse); the die-axis (relative rotation between obverse and reverse) varies by 0-360 degrees. Ancient coins show 10% variation around standard orientation; modern coins are constrained to standard (180°) or medallic (0°). The die-axis distribution across many strikes from the same die-pair reveals striking-technique variance
llm_application: Diagnose LLM inference variance by measuring the 'die-axis' analog: the relative rotation between two complementary outputs (e.g., forward generation vs reverse-conditioned regeneration of the same content). Across many inference passes from the same prompt-pair, the die-axis distribution diagnoses inference-stack variance. The mechanism is a structural diagnostic with no analog in current variance measures (which use scalar perplexity or entropy).
novelty_claim: Existing inference variance uses scalar metrics. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work imports a die-axis (relative rotation between dual outputs) distributional diagnostic for inference variance.
